ogTitle: Fitzroy Crossing vs Floreat: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Fitzroy Crossing and Floreat.
heading: Fitzroy Crossing vs Floreat — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Fitzroy Crossing, Western Australia and Floreat, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Fitzroy Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Floreat (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Fitzroy Crossing or Floreat safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Fitzroy Crossing has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Floreat scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Fitzroy Crossing scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Floreat scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Fitzroy Crossing or Floreat?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Fitzroy Crossing has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
